findings.claim: Shared-key ownership ('does THIS surface render+bind THIS shared key?') is now centralized behind one internal pure non-reactive helper partition_view (R/paintr-partition-view.R, ADR 0026 rank 11) — LANDED in b6a2f72, no longer a parked proposal: three surface roles (ptr_partition_roles = c('single','panel_host','instance')) — 'single' (single-instance L1, owns all keys + renders its inline shared section), 'panel_host' (L2 standalone panel, owns the >=2-formula panel keys), 'instance' (embedded ptr_server, defers panel keys to the host) — each construct a view via new_partition_view(role, panel_keys, consumer_keys) and query partition_host_owned_keys()/partition_renders_section(), replacing the three previously hand-derived host_owned_keys complement sets + the render_shared_section boolean; the partition ALGORITHM (shared_partition in paintr-shared-coordinator.R) is unchanged — only the consumption side was unified.
findings.detail: This is the channel where the recurring 2026-05-22 partition-binder bug lived. Parked (ADR 0026) as a design abstraction for an interactive session, not landed.
findings.verification: Verified by symbol against current source. The partition_view module lives at R/paintr-partition-view.R (tracked; created in commit b6a2f72 'centralize shared-key ownership behind partition_view (ADR 0026 #11)'), defining ptr_partition_roles=c('single','panel_host','instance'), new_partition_view(role,panel_keys,consumer_keys), partition_host_owned_keys(view), partition_renders_section(view). The header comment matches the claim verbatim ('three hand-derived host_owned_keys complement sets plus a parallel render_shared_section boolean ... the 2026-05-22 partition-binder bug'). Consumption sites confirmed: R/paintr-app.R:169 (render_shared_section=partition_renders_section(new_partition_view('single'))), :339 (host_owned_keys=partition_host_owned_keys(new_partition_view('single'))), :1166-1167 (new_partition_view('instance',panel_keys=names(dots$shared))); R/paintr-shared-ui.R:416-417 (panel_host). shared_partition() still lives in R/paintr-shared-coordinator.R:28, unchanged (algorithm side). Cited file is the renamed paintr-partition-view.R (the stale source string listed paintr-shared-coordinator.R/-shared-ui.R/-app.R); the claim holds and is LANDED, not parked.

findings.claim: ggpaintr has TWO deliberately separate R-name normalizers: the column-vector normalizer (ptr_normalize_column_name_vector — allows dots, pads/truncates to length n, make.unique across columns) and the single binding-name normalizer (ptr_make_valid_name — collapse non-[alnum_] to _, trim, fallback-if-empty, reserved-word suffix, make.names).
findings.detail: ptr_make_valid_name now backs BOTH the file-derived default name (ptr_upload_default_name = thin wrapper) and the typed-shortcut name (the else branch in ptr_resolve_upload_info), killing the prior drift between those two binding-name paths. The column-vector normalizer is intentionally NOT folded in: distinct job (dot-bearing names like Sepal.Length, cross-column make.unique, length padding) whose outputs are pinned by many tests; the upload-default-name path has its own exact-output tests (test-upload.R / test-coverage-gaps.R, e.g. "my-data.csv"->"my_data", "if.csv"->"if_").
findings.verification: Confirmed two distinct normalizers: ptr_normalize_column_name_vector (data.R:77-107; allows dots, pads/truncates to n, make.unique) vs ptr_make_valid_name (upload.R:15-27; no dots, trim, fallback, reserved-word suffix, make.names).

findings.claim: The implementable pre-commit hook blocks any commit touching files matching `dev/plans/*.html | dev/plans/*.md | *-plan.md` unless they carry a current `implementable: PASS` stamp — and the `*-plan.md` glob matches files ANYWHERE, e.g. tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md, not just under dev/plans/.
findings.detail: ADRs under dev/adr/ are NOT gated. Bit this session: a cosmetic edit to tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md blocked the commit; fix was `git restore --source=HEAD --staged --worktree` that file (a plain `git checkout --` left it staged). Avoid editing any *-plan.md during refactors.
findings.source: dev/githooks/pre-commit; observed: editing tests/browser/browser-test-plan.md blocked a commit
findings.verification: Confirmed dev/githooks/pre-commit line 26 case-globs 'dev/plans/*.html|dev/plans/*.md|*-plan.md' requiring a current 'implementable: PASS' stamp (content-hash matched); the *-plan.md glob matches anywhere, not only under dev/plans/.

findings.claim: "x is a usable R binding symbol" is now the single predicate is_syntactic_name(x) = is.character(x) && length(x)==1L && !is.na(x) && nzchar(x) && make.names(x)==x; it was open-coded ~13 times across the source-binding helpers and bound-name cleanup.
findings.detail: Reserved words fail because make.names appends a dot (make.names("if")=="if." != "if"). The !is.na guard only affects NA input, which no call site supplies. as.character(symbol) is always a length-1 non-NA character so the partial-form sites (nzchar(cand) && make.names(cand)==cand) are equivalent under is_syntactic_name.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-server.R:1103-1106: is_syntactic_name <- function(x) { is.character(x) && length(x)==1L && !is.na(x) && nzchar(x) && make.names(x)==x }. Body matches the claimed predicate exactly. Confirmed it is the single predicate: 14 occurrences in paintr-server.R and 0 elsewhere in R/ (grep), called at the binding-symbol-cleanup sites (1032,1139,1145,1232,1238,1267,1402,1412,1419). Extracted in commit 9bb8235 'refactor(server): extract is_syntactic_name() — one binding-symbol predicate'.

findings.claim: In ptr_setup_pipelines the two panel-owned source observer blocks were identical EXCEPT the bound_names key — the bare-data-layer branch binds under the LAYER name, the pipeline-head branch binds under the SOURCE id — so the extracted wire_panel_owned_source(state, key, node, entry, session, slot) takes `key` explicitly to preserve that duality.
findings.detail: Corroborates the existing bound-names-key-duality understanding. is_panel_owned_id(panel_sources, id) = !is.null(id) && id %in% names(panel_sources %||% list()) replaced ~9 open-coded membership tests; it takes the panel_sources list directly so both state$panel_sources and a local panel_sources call sites share it.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-server.R: wire_panel_owned_source(state, key, node, entry, session, slot) at L1167 takes `key` explicitly; its doc comment (L1159-1166) states verbatim 'the bare-data-layer branch passes the LAYER name, the pipeline-head branch passes the SOURCE id (the bound_names key duality)'. is_panel_owned_id(panel_sources, id) at L1155-1157 = !is.null(id) && id %in% names(panel_sources %||% list()), with comment 'Accepts the panel_sources list directly so both state$panel_sources and a local panel_sources call sites share one check'. Both halves of the claim hold.

findings.claim: The parallel full-suite gate (TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE TESTTHAT_CPUS=4) reproducibly flakes assertions in the consumer-seeding / derived-column browser cluster (set_sentinel boot-tail race) that PASS when those same files are re-run serially — boot-tail races, not product failures; the per-commit landing gate must be run serially (devtools::test(), FAIL 0 / ERROR 0 / SKIP 0; current serial PASS ≈ 3635 and growing — 0/0/0 is the gate, never the count). Cluster files: test-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-consumer-deselect-stays-empty.R, test-default-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R.
findings.detail: Corroborates the shinytest2-boot-tail-race understanding. Baseline this session: parallel PASS 3433 with 6 such failures; serial re-run of those 4 files FAIL 0 / PASS 15.
findings.source: TESTTHAT_PARALLEL=TRUE devtools::test() vs serial; test-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-consumer-deselect-stays-empty.R, test-default-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R
findings.verification: Behavioral claim re-checked vs CURRENT source + orientation: all 4 named cluster files exist (tests/testthat/test-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R, test-consumer-deselect-stays-empty.R, test-default-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R). Parallel boot-tail flakes in the consumer-seeding/derived-column cluster (set_sentinel boot-tail race) are CONFIRMED current behavior; helper-super-pressure.R was hardened this session. The serial-landing-gate guidance still holds. Only the embedded PASS counts drifted: finding cited ~3439/parallel 3433; CLAUDE.md authoritative-gate now says last ≈3388 (2026-05-29) and current full suite is ~3635 (serial earlier ~3545). 0/0/0 remains the gate, not the count.

findings.claim: The renderUI-container output id for ANY placeholder (value, source, or consumer) is `paste0(raw_id, "_ui")`, now defined exactly once as `placeholder_output_id()` in R/paintr-ids.R.
findings.detail: Previously three byte-identical helpers — value_output_id/source_output_id (paintr-build-ui.R) and consumer_output_id (paintr-ids.R). The widget binds at the raw id; its renderUI container is `<raw_id>_ui` to avoid an inputId/container collision. emit_placeholder_rows()'s former 3-branch role dispatch collapsed to one call since all roles produced the same id.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-ids.R:139-141: placeholder_output_id <- function(raw_id) paste0(raw_id, '_ui'). Its comment names it the 'Single source of truth for the _ui suffix contract' for ANY placeholder (value/source/consumer). Confirmed all roles route through it: R/paintr-build-ui.R:63 (value), :70 (consumer), :85 (source) all call placeholder_output_id(node$id); emit_placeholder_rows at R/paintr-input-spec.R:339 calls placeholder_output_id(raw_id); server.R uses it at 2184/2259/2324/2696/3009. Consolidated in commit 9604978 'refactor(ids): consolidate 3 identical *_output_id helpers into placeholder_output_id'.

findings.claim: ptr_app and ptr_app_grid are ALREADY thin wrappers — each just calls its distinct *_components builder then shiny::shinyApp(ui=parts$ui, server=parts$server); the only shared line is the shinyApp call, so there is no real L1-assembly duplication to extract (deep-leave).
findings.detail: ptr_app_components (single formula -> ptr_translate -> ptr_build_app_ui + ptr_make_app_server) and ptr_app_grid_components (multi-plot grid) are genuinely different assembly, not copies.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-app.R. ptr_app (L134-152) calls ptr_app_components() then shiny::shinyApp(ui=parts$ui, server=parts$server). Serena overview confirms ptr_app_grid and ptr_app_grid_components are separate symbols in paintr-app.R (distinct *_components builders). The single-formula assembly (ptr_app_components -> ptr_translate + ptr_build_app_ui + ptr_make_app_server) differs genuinely from the grid path, so the only shared line is the shinyApp call — claim holds (deep-leave, no L1-assembly duplication).

findings.claim: The exported argument-validator factories are ptr_arg_string / ptr_arg_numeric / ptr_arg_symbol / ptr_arg_symbol_or_string / ptr_arg_expression (no ptr_default_* aliases). The old ptr_arg_numeric_vector was removed — vector validation is now ptr_arg_numeric(vector = TRUE, length = NULL) — and ptr_arg_symbol() was added. Each returns a validator closure function(arg_expr) -> canonical | abort for a keyword's formula-time argument (a validator, not a coercer/default value). The roxygen doc group is @name/@rdname ptr_arg_validators (man/ptr_arg_validators.Rd; no ptr_default_args.Rd).
findings.detail: They return a validator closure function(arg_expr)->canonical|abort for a keyword's formula-time argument, NOT a coercer/default value — hence "arg" not "default". Used at the call site as `positional_arg = ptr_arg_numeric()`. man/ptr_default_args.Rd removed, ptr_arg_validators.Rd added by devtools::document().
findings.verification: Re-checked R/paintr-default-args.R symbol overview + NAMESPACE + man/. CURRENT exported factory set is exactly ptr_arg_string / ptr_arg_numeric / ptr_arg_symbol / ptr_arg_symbol_or_string / ptr_arg_expression (NAMESPACE:90-94). ptr_arg_numeric_vector NO LONGER EXISTS — it was folded into ptr_arg_numeric(vector = FALSE, length = NULL) (body at lines 287-314: validates a single numeric literal when !vector, a non-NA numeric vector of optional fixed length when vector=TRUE), and a NEW factory ptr_arg_symbol() was added. The roxygen doc group is @name/@rdname ptr_arg_validators (lines 53, 230, 252, 269, 286, 320) and man/ptr_arg_validators.Rd is the only Rd (no ptr_default_args.Rd, no ptr_arg_numeric_vector.Rd). So the rename-away-from ptr_default_* and the @rdname holds, but the finding's enumerated factory list (which names ptr_arg_numeric_vector) is drifted to the current set.

findings.claim: The three public placeholder constructors (ptr_define_placeholder_value/_consumer/_source) are now thin wrappers over one internal `ptr_define_core(role, ...)` that owns the shared ~20-line validate-then-register prologue; role-specific bits (build_ui arity, resolve hook, validate_input/shortcut acceptance, runtime default, data_aware) branch on `role`.
findings.detail: build_ui validation args = c("node","cols","data") for consumer else c("node"); source validates resolve_data + defaults resolve_expr to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value) + validates the `shortcut` logical; value/consumer validate resolve_expr and accept validate_input; runtime default is identity for value/consumer and an abort-guard ("only meaningful inside ptr_app()") for source.
findings.verification: Read ptr_define_core (R/paintr-registry.R:354-423, include_body) and the three public wrappers (ptr_define_placeholder_value @597, _consumer @720, _source @878 — each is a thin wrapper that forwards to ptr_define_core(role, ...)). The shared prologue holds exactly: ensure_registry_initialized + validate_keyword + validate_keyword_no_shadow; build_ui_args = c("node","cols","data") when role=="consumer" else "node"; for role=="source" it validates resolve_data, defaults resolve_expr to function(value,node,...) rlang::sym(value), and validates the shortcut logical; otherwise (value/consumer) it validates resolve_expr and validate_session_input (c("value","ctx")); runtime default = abort-guard ("`<kw>()` is only meaningful inside `ptr_app()`.") for source else embellish_identity(); data_aware = role %in% c("consumer","source"). Public constructor args are the ADR-0027 names (parse_positional_arg, parse_named_args, validate_session_input, embellish_eval, ui_text_defaults). The claim/detail match current source verbatim.

findings.claim: After ADR 0027 the placeholder public ARGUMENT names are parse_positional_arg / ui_text_defaults (also parse_named_args, validate_session_input, embellish_eval) but the registry-RECORD storage keys deliberately stay entry$default_arg / entry$copy_defaults (and entry$named_args, entry$validate_input, entry$runtime) — the public arg name and the internal key differ on purpose so downstream readers (translate/server/copy) were untouched. validate_default_arg was renamed validate_positional_arg (its message names `parse_positional_arg`); validate_copy_defaults kept its function name but its messages now name `ui_text_defaults`. Any code/test reading entry$default_arg / entry$copy_defaults still works.
findings.detail: default_arg->positional_arg (validates the keyword's single positional formula argument, e.g. the 50 in ppPct(50)); copy_defaults->ui_text_defaults (ties to the public ui_text vocabulary). validate_default_arg was renamed to validate_positional_arg and its message; validate_copy_defaults kept its function name but its messages now say "ui_text_defaults". Any code or test reading entry$default_arg/entry$copy_defaults still works.
findings.verification: Read ptr_define_core entry list (R/paintr-registry.R:354-423) and the value wrapper signature (597-611). The deliberate public-arg vs record-key divergence HOLDS and is now even stronger after ADR 0027: the entry list stores validate_input = validate_session_input, default_arg = parse_positional_arg, named_args = parse_named_args, runtime = runtime_fn (from embellish_eval), copy_defaults = ui_text_defaults. So internal record keys (default_arg, named_args, validate_input, runtime, copy_defaults) are KEPT while the public constructor args were renamed. BUT the finding names the public arg as `positional_arg`; the CURRENT public arg is `parse_positional_arg` (the ADR-0027 final name) — see the wrapper signature (line 599) and validate_positional_arg's abort message (218-223) which says "`parse_positional_arg`". Also: validate_default_arg was renamed to validate_positional_arg (param named positional_arg); validate_copy_defaults kept its function name and its messages now name ui_text_defaults (309-311). Drifted only on the public-arg label — rewrite positional_arg -> parse_positional_arg.

findings.claim: The single-instance and panel-host source paths already SHARE resolve_upload_source (the panel-host path calls the same master impl), so the genuine remaining cross-file divergence is only the renderUI bodies + the seeding-decision closures/latches + the spec normalization — and those differ by reactive dependencies (single-instance is state-backed; the host runs state=NULL per ADR 0006), so a forced full collapse would couple them artificially.
findings.detail: There is NO apply_spec_at_boot_host twin in source — apply_spec_at_boot exists in server.R; the host spec handling is a separate small loop in shared-ui.R (~line 750). Refines the prior "two parallel source-handling paths" finding: the resolution CORE is deduped; the divergence is reactive-shape, not copy-paste.
findings.verification: resolve_upload_source is defined once in R/paintr-server.R:1307 (resolve_upload_source <- function(input_slot, shortcut_slot, node, entry, ...)); its doc comment (L1275-1286) states 'Single resolver for upload-source mechanics, two call sites today (bare-data-source layer loop and pipeline-head source loop, both in ptr_setup_pipelines()) and a third forthcoming via PLAN-04 (ptr_setup_panel_sources() at host scope)' — confirming the resolution CORE is shared. apply_spec_at_boot is defined at paintr-server.R:546; apply_spec_at_boot_host is a SEPARATE symbol at R/paintr-shared-ui.R:753 — confirming there is no apply_spec_at_boot_host twin in server.R and the host spec handling is separate. Claim holds.

findings.claim: The three source binding-name derivations are genuinely DIFFERENT per call site (not safely collapsible into one binding_name_for_source): try_bind_source_default splits lookup_name vs binding_name (post-PLAN-02 the empty-shortcut path looks up under the user name but BINDS under node$auto_name), feeds resolve_expr `node$default`; panel_owned_binding_name feeds resolve_expr the resolved `df`; try_bind_source_default_resolved takes the resolved shortcut and feeds resolve_expr `file_info`; auto-name fallback conditions differ per ADR 0024/0025/0023.
findings.detail: A unified derivor would be a shallow multi-flag pass-through and would risk behaviour change in the seeding-sensitive path. The only genuinely shared kernel across them is the syntactic-name validation predicate.
findings.verification: Read R/paintr-server.R. All three derivations exist and differ per site: try_bind_source_default (L1201) splits lookup_name vs binding_name — empty-shortcut path binds under node$auto_name (via panel_source_canonical_name, L1264-1270) while looking up under the user name, feeds resolve_expr node$default (L1234); panel_owned_binding_name (L1112) feeds resolve_expr the resolved df (L1141: entry$resolve_expr(df, node)); try_bind_source_default_resolved is a distinct symbol at L1447 taking the resolved shortcut. Auto-name fallback conditions differ per ADR 0024/0025 (comments L1129-1135, L1259-1270). The only shared kernel is is_syntactic_name (called in all three). Claim holds.

findings.claim: The verb-derived param_override (`{param}` -> "verb()") and " in verb()" label_suffix for a pipeline-stage placeholder are now derived once by stage_overrides_for_entry(entry, drop_suffix); both the static build path and the renderUI re-derivation path call it.
findings.detail: drop_suffix = !is.na(entry$stage_id) at the render-time site (suffix suppressed when an enclosing stage-group header already names the verb). Post-PLAN-01 the panel-level args no longer reach invoke_build_ui, which is why the renderUI body must re-derive — that re-derivation was the duplication.
findings.verification: Confirmed stage_overrides_for_entry(entry,drop_suffix) (build-ui.R:282-293) returns param_override=verb() and label_suffix=' in verb()'; both build_pipeline_stage_ui (static) and pipeline_override_for_node (renderUI re-derive) call it.

findings.claim: On any commit that changes R/, the pre-commit hook regenerates and auto-stages dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/symbol-lines.json (it prints "[pre-commit] R/ changed — rebuilding symbol-lines.json"); use `git add -A` so the regenerated file is included.
findings.verification: Read dev/githooks/pre-commit (lines 72-79: orchestrator hook delegates to the sidecar `sh dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/pre-commit-symbol-manifest.sh || exit 1`) and the sidecar itself. The sidecar: stages-detects R/*.R via `git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=AM | grep -E '^R/.*\.R$'`; on a hit prints exactly `[pre-commit] R/ changed — rebuilding symbol-lines.json...`; runs Rscript dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/build-symbol-manifest.R (abort on failure); then `git add dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/symbol-lines.json` to land the refreshed JSON in the same commit. core.hooksPath = dev/githooks confirmed. symbol-lines.json present (63KB). Mechanism holds verbatim.

findings.claim: An automated duplication/refactor sweep systematically OVER-states duplication — multiple ggpaintr candidates flagged as copy-paste were on inspection either per-site-different (source binding-name derivation), an already-shared single helper with N callers (collect_orphan_shared_stages, resolve_upload_source), or already-thin wrappers (ptr_app/ptr_app_grid); the discipline that held is to run the deletion test on each candidate and extract only the genuinely-identical kernel (e.g. the predicate, not the derivation).
findings.detail: Concrete refinements made: #5 scoped to param_override (orphan-stage left); #7 reduced to is_syntactic_name (derivation left); #2 left the column-vector normalizer separate; #8 preserved the layer/source key duality; #12 deep-left entirely.
findings.source: this session's candidate verification vs the explorer-synthesis catalog (ADR 0026)
findings.verification: Meta-finding corroborated by independently verifying its cited concrete cases against current source: (#7) source binding-name derivation reduced to is_syntactic_name with the per-site derivations LEFT separate — confirmed (finding source-binding-derivation-per-site: three distinct derivors share only is_syntactic_name at R/paintr-server.R:1103); (resolve_upload_source / collect_orphan_shared_stages) already-shared single helper with N callers — confirmed resolve_upload_source defined once at R/paintr-server.R:1307 with two+ call sites, and collect_orphan_shared_stages defined once at R/paintr-build-ui.R:508 with callers at build-ui.R:543, server.R:1814, app.R:384; (ptr_app/ptr_app_grid) already-thin wrappers — confirmed (finding ptr-app-grid-already-thin); (#5) scoped to param_override (orphan-stage left) — param_override is a live arg threaded through build-ui.R (L51,209,287,818,825). The cited refs drifted (build-ui.R:508 is now collect_orphan_shared_stages, not param_override; server.R:1268-1300 is the resolve_upload_source doc block) but every concrete claim resolves to real current code. The discipline lesson (run the deletion test per candidate; extract only the genuinely-identical kernel) holds.
